reviews.review_msg: Razvan was great in Set Theory! Lectures were a bit heavy but they drew directly from the textbook (free online) so it was easy to review concepts. He was friendly and helpful during office hours. The quiz and exam questions were very similar to the homework, very fair exams. He also clearly cared about the students' course experience.

(For Set Theory). Good, clear lectures with a reasonable pace. Quizzes and exams were all fair. If you did the homework you will easily pass them. Textbook is old and some of the proofs in it skip tons of steps I did not find trivial. Highlight is the tutorials. This semester it was led by a TA who absolutely carried my grade. Very worth attending!

Super good Prof for lin alg. I was worried about this course at first, but it ended up being quite straightforward and interesting! His exams are super fair, you have nothing to worry about if you do the HW. Quiz questions pulled straight from assignments. Solid lecturer and gives good balance of proofs and calculation/application!

Dr Fetecau gives clear explanations and is well-organized. Exams were fair. Goes quite fast in class, a bit difficult to keep up. Doesn't upload notes. Be ready to spend a lot of time studying for this course. I supplemented it with reading/practicing from the textbook closely and also watching videos that explain the intuition behind the concepts.

The course is bit challenging than Calculus but easy if you put efforts. The quizzes were straight out of homework assignments. We had two proof assignments which were pretty average. The exams weren't that difficult. Dr.Fetecau was really helpful and is a really good prof. He doesn't upload lecture notes so you need to go to the lectures.
Felt uncomfortable approaching him, did not care about students success. Very tough grader, would avoid this professor
unit test are from text book questions, but doesn't post any notes or practice exams on canvas. The only ways to get them is to attend his lectures. He doesn't care about students.

He's an average professor; he teaches in detail but he doesn't post any notes on Canvas. If you miss a lecture, the only way to get notes is from other people. We have OQ every week, about 15 question each. He gives a unit test on every chapter, totaling six tests. The final exam is weighted at 50 percent, which is a lot compared to other courses.

Solid prof. He writes by hand in his lectures, which makes it easy to follow along. He does go a bit fast though so make sure you pay attention. His exams are fair and he's a cool guy.
Also,make sure to do all his assigned textbook questions, since his quizzes are pulled directly from those assignments.

(Review for MATH 240 - there's no option for 240 at the moment)
Razvan Fetecau has been great so far for Math 240. Gives clear lectures and explain concepts nicely. Midterm was fair and similar to what was done in class. Quizzes are directly from homework questions, so make sure to complete them early.
Not very clear in teaching and makes it hard to follow along
Extremely clear and thorough lectures. Homework could be long and hard. Exams were fair.

Expects a lot but is quite reasonable in terms of expectations. Like nearly all math classes, you'll need to put in work in order to do well. Textbook is a little lacking in terms of information, so you'll either need to attend all lectures or be disciplined in teaching the material yourself. Very helpful office hours.

Extremely picky when it comes to grading. Whole course feels like you're being punished from not getting it rather than reaching out to students and helping them learn. Course would've been better if he acquired some educational knowledge.

His notes are extremely unclear and unreadable outside of class. Exams are hard and he is very picky when it comes to marking. You get this feeling that he is just teaching because it's his job, not because he likes it. He seemed to be unfamiliar with the homework he assigned, and lecture will leave you confused.

Explains complex ideas well. Assignments are tough; exams are hard, though fair. Lectures were quite interesting, if overly abstract; perhaps, though, that's only to be expected in a special topics course.

If you have a chance to take a course with Dr. Fetecau, do it. It is the unique mind blowing journey into the magic of MATH. Dr. Fetecau is well-prepared for every lecture, highly skilled in conveying his knowledge to his students, and cares a lot to help students get that knowledge. Home works are not easy, but very useful. One of the best profs!
Assignments were hard. But he's very helpful. I worked hard and was a regular to his office hour. So I did good on assignments and I think exams were fair.
Good class. You need to work very hard though.

No doubt calculus is hard. Coming from a math background, i can telll his finals were NOT from a mathematics respecting person. He is a perfect example of how education in big universities is all money making process or its just all about cramming. DO NOT take math 251 with him.
Really easy to get lost during lectures, and he seems to think everything is trivially easy.

Dr. Fetecau is extremely well-versed in the subjects he lectures, which permits a more penetrating investigation of the material. His courses are very well structured and immerse you in a mathematical journey. Very good prof
Hard midterms and final.He himself thinks they're easy though -_-. For some reason I don't think he had his mic on the whole semester
I would avoid him at all costs.

As the comments say below, his assignments are numerical, and exams are completely conceptual. You have no way of even preparing for exams, they're absolutely terrible. Avoid Razvan at all costs until he gains more experience teaching, and has a wake-up call regarding how difficult his tests are.

I would not recommend taking a class from him. He is helpful and a good guy but his exams are ridiculous compared to the material he covers in class. Exams are full of proofs and conceptual questions when these topics are not really covered in the course. Even if you do all the problems he assigned the exams are brutal.
Played soccer with him over the summer semester, didnt know he was a proff till after. Very good soccer player, we scored many goals.

THE MOST terrible professor i have seen in sfu, alway makes the quiz and exam too difficult. especilly the final, is totally different than the sample. holy sh*t. if you want to take math, he will kill your GPA

First off, this guy is a genius. He is hard & unsympathetic but is very clear and concise, perfect notes, hard mid-terms(50%avg on the first, 40% on the second) & final. He is very approachable/helpful in his office hours. You really have to be on your game to get a good mark with this prof. He's basically like "Get on my level, son, or perish".

averages from both midterms were a fail. he scaled them down both times... he does a lot of examples but students don't get the theory, and then he seems surprised that people fail his tests.. yeah good math teachers are a hard find, but he is not one of them
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOORING!! he rushes through the material by doing examples from the text.. no imagination.
